subtasks.description: Evaluate whether the developer's request to access the production database for live debugging complies with organizational access control policy. Determine if live debugging is an approved use case, what approval levels are required, and what conditions (time-boxing, audit logging, etc.) must be met.

plan.cost_rationale: Single policy_action subtask at mid tier because the request requires evaluating a governance decision (production database access for debugging) against an access control and security policy registry. This is not a simple yes/no rule but likely involves multiple policy dimensions: access control (who can access prod), use-case approval (is debugging allowed), approval workflows (what sign-offs are needed), and conditions (audit requirements, time windows). Mid tier is appropriate for reasoning across multiple policy signals and potential exceptions or conditions.

output.reason: dev-alice does not hold the on-call role and has not obtained Manager approval, violating POL-401. Additionally, POL-402 explicitly restricts debug access to the designated staging environment, not production. Both policies independently deny this request. The developer should be redirected to the staging environment for debugging activities.
applied_constraints: Manager approval required before any production database access can be granted
On-call role assignment required in addition to Manager approval per POL-401
Debugging activities must be performed exclusively in the designated staging environment per POL-402
Access to prod-mysql-primary is denied for query_execute under current user role and approval status
If production access becomes necessary after proper approvals, time-boxed sessions with full audit logging must be enforced
